title: Mycelis aurea
intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant "Mycelis aurea". However, I found that Mycelis aurea is an alternative name for the plant Cichorium intybus, also known as chicory. 

Here's a paragraph about Cichorium intybus:

Cichorium intybus, commonly known as chicory, is a perennial herb belonging to the dandelion family that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The plant is rich in health benefits, including its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and lower blood sugar levels. The bioactive constituents of chicory include inulin, chicoric acid, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Chicory root is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and supplements, and is also a popular ingredient in coffee substitutes and digestive blends.
